notes: Structural drying in hotels/lodging. Show real drying equipment deployed by a professional (air movers, refrigerant dehumidifiers, moisture meters) plus the taught content-protection method (sealed polyethylene bins for linens). Upscale, calm, bright. Australian, metric. No on-image text, no logos, no guests.

images.context: Course hero — drying equipment deployed in an elegant Australian hotel guest suite
images.prompt: A water-damage restoration technician in gloves deploying air movers and a refrigerant dehumidifier across the carpet of an elegant, empty Australian hotel guest suite after water damage, equipment neatly positioned along the skirting, curtains open to soft natural daylight. Clean, upscale, calm, professional. Realistic photography, wide mid-distance framing.

images.context: Module 3 — moisture-meter reading in a hotel corridor during phased drying
images.prompt: Over-the-shoulder close-up of a technician's gloved hand holding a handheld moisture meter against the wall near the skirting board in a refined Australian hotel corridor, reading moisture content, an air mover drying the patterned carpet nearby. Hands-on-task framing, methodical. Realistic photography.

images.context: Module 2 — protecting porous linens and upholstery in sealed polyethylene bins
images.prompt: A restoration professional in gloves carefully packing unaffected hotel linens, bedding and upholstery cushions into sealed clear polyethylene bins and bags to protect them from cross-contamination during drying, in a bright, orderly Australian hotel room, an inventory clipboard resting on the bed. Careful, organised, professional, natural light. Realistic photography, mid-distance framing.
